title: Family Support Centers/Outreach * At Risk Families - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Family Support Centers/Outreach * At Risk Families 14 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter At Risk Families Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support through home visits, resource navigation, and respite child care for families in immediate need of help. Families involved in outreach also have access to other Relief Nursery services. 1720 W 25th Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Low-income families with children age 6 and younger who are experiencing multiple stressors Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm (541) 343-9706 111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY ROBIN JAQUA CHILD AND FAMILY CENTER RELIEF NURSERY OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support through home visits, resource navigation, and respite child care for families in immediate need of help. Families involved in outreach also have access to other Relief Nursery services. 850 S 42nd Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Low-income families with children age 6 and younger who are experiencing multiple stressors Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm (541) 343-9706 111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers comprehensive family support for families with children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Services include therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, family support and individualized case management, home visiting, parenting education, and group activities designed to strengthen parenting skills and promote healthy child development 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Young children ages 0-5 who are experiencing high levels of stress or who have experienced trauma and/or are at risk for abuse and neglect; parents who have multiple risk factors and are struggling to nurture and care for their children. High-risk families typically experience several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single parenthood, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, history with drugs and alcohol, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW GLADSTONE CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nurseries serve families with young children who are most at risk of abuse and neglect. They provide high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. LifeWorks NW provides wraparound services to high-risk families. Services include early childhood intervention and social, emotional, and family support. Also provides mental health services and trauma assessments, traumatic stress expertise, home visitations, parent/infant and parent/toddler classes, art and music therapy, respite child care, and weekly parenting classes using the "Making Parenting a Pleasure" curriculum. Parents/children must be enrolled to receive most services. 18907 Portland Avenue Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Caregivers with children ages 0-5 and women in their 3rd trimester of pregnancy Serves high-risk families. "High-risk" typically means experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent households, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: By appointment Email (541) 416-0146 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OLD MILL CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nursery providing services for at-risk families that include therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers, home visiting, outreach and parent support groups. Resource closet available with food, children's clothing, diapers, wipes, hygiene items and cleaning supplies. 1650 SW 45th Place Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 who are low-income, a single-parent, teen parent, experiencing mental health or drug/alcohol issues, victims of trauma or domestic violence, involvement with child welfare, homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (541) 757-8068 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TREASURE VALLEY CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves families with children who are at risk of abuse and neglect by providing intensive support through individualized case management. Also provides parenting education, home visits, developmental assessments, group activities, and referrals to other community resources. Mental health care, counseling for domestic violence, and support for adults recovering from addiction are offered as well. 780 SE 6th Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: -High-risk families with children ages 0-5. Families with children younger than 18 months are served through home visits only -High-risk typically means families experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent household, criminal history, adult survivor of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Classroom sessions: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday. No classes most major holidays including Christmas break. Email (541) 823-2526 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY EAST PORTLAND FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting education, support through home visits, and group activities and intensive case management. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 2609 SE 136th Avenue Portland, OR 97236 Eligibility: High risk families with children ages 6 weeks to 5 years old. Intakes are completed to determine eligibility.
